title: Fresh start with no persisted token redirects to login stub screen

preconditions.description: Expo web app is served at http://localhost:8081 via npm run web.
No 'auth_token' key is present in localStorage (fresh browser context, no prior session).

journey.notes: Opens the app at the session restore splash (app/index.tsx). A loading indicator should appear briefly.
Session restore reads no token from SecureStore and calls router.replace('/(auth)/login'). On web, SecureStore maps to localStorage; key 'auth_token' is absent so redirect fires immediately.

assertions.notes: Route must contain 'login', confirming Expo Router landed on /(auth)/login.
The login stub screen renders text containing 'Login'. Milestone 1 stub text is 'Login — Milestone 2'.
Client home must not be visible — unauthenticated user must not reach the guarded client area.
Companion home must not be visible — unauthenticated user must not reach the guarded companion area.

notes: This test uses a fresh browser context so localStorage has no 'auth_token'. Playwright's browser context isolation achieves this by default.
The loading screen is transient and does not need a separate assertion — the route change to /login is sufficient evidence that session restore ran correctly.
login.tsx in M01 is a stub rendering static text. The exact text 'Login — Milestone 2' is acceptable as the observable assertion value.
